violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
An employee was observed rinsing a blender in (front) handwashing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the storage shelves, countertops and trash shoot (cook line) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility (kitchen, breakroom, walk-in cooler, dry storage room, beneath ice machine, etc.) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment (ovens) or items were observed in the (breakroom and dry storage room area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (lemon slicer and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Sliced tomatoes and pancaked batter were observed held between 50-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated tomatoes to walk-in cooler and discarded batter. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food in the kitchen area. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025
Inspector observed a stack of clean plastic contains with condensation buildup. Equipment and utensils must be properly air-dried prior to stacking. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -Plate storage holder, -Cooking equipment and cold hold drawers, -Hood vents above cooking equipment and dishwasher, -Prep units throughout, -Grill scraper, -Cart in storage closet. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025
Inspector observed heavy grease buildup on the outside of the grease receptacle. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

The floors in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Container of strawberries and chemical spray bottle were observed in the hand-washing sink in servers station. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (hand slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
Utensils(ice cream server) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces: 1.all prep units 2.food storage shelves on cook line 3.hand washing sink on cook line 4.under grill drawers 5.plate holder 6.under fryolators have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

The floors and floor grates throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024
